rationale.R1.1: The section carries an explicit gap statement (miscalibrated UQ under slow-converging nuisance learners), a named contribution (CalDML), and a 'first procedure' thesis — all must be reconstructed and verified for internal coherence.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain (problem → mechanism → related-work contrast → empirical positioning) must be checked: the claim that exchangeability alone suffices where prior rate conditions are needed is a logical crux that could break the introduction's logic if unsupported.
rationale.R1.4: The section asserts CalDML is 'the first procedure' of its kind and frames it as a 'meaningful step beyond the existing toolbox.' These novelty claims must be assessed against the four cited prior methods (Robins & Rotnitzky, Chernozhukov et al., Wager & Athey, Künzel et al., Kennedy) to verify the gap is real and not overstated.
rationale.R1.6: A section-level review almost universally includes sentence and paragraph quality; the prose here is dense and technical, warranting attention to clarity and register.
rationale.R1.7: Six specific citations are named with author, year, and attributed contributions (e.g., Kennedy 2023's rate conditions, Smith and Todd 2005's dataset framing); accuracy and completeness of attribution are directly reviewable from the text.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize substantive technical critique — assessing logical validity of the calibration argument, strength of the novelty claim, and citation accuracy — or editorial/writing quality — sentence clarity, prose flow, and paragraph structure?
